metadata.question: Q20
metadata.methodology: 20 Newsgroups, 3 architectures, 90 clusters (30 pure + 30 mixed + 30 random), purity ground truth, Steiger test + cross-validated R^2 + nested AIC

pre_registered_criteria.NOT_TAUTOLOGICAL: Steiger p < 0.05 (R_full > E) on >= 2/3 architectures AND cross-validated R^2(R_full) > R^2(E) on >= 2/3 architectures
pre_registered_criteria.TAUTOLOGICAL: Steiger p >= 0.05 on all 3 architectures AND |CV_R^2(R_full) - CV_R^2(E)| < 0.01 on all 3

audit_fixes: BUG-1: Dropped 8e conservation (Df*alpha=2 trivially under v2 defs)
STAT-1: Replaced 0.05 rho threshold with Steiger Z-test
METH-3: Added nested model comparison via AIC
METH-4: Mixed clusters now have continuous purity variation

verdict.explanation: R_full significantly outperforms E alone via Steiger's test on 3/3 architectures (p < 0.05), and cross-validated R^2(R_full) > R^2(E) on 3/3 architectures.
nuance: The grad_S (dispersion) component adds significant predictive value beyond E alone, as measured by AIC improvement on 3/3 architectures.
The sigma^Df (fractal scaling) term adds measurable value on 2/3 architectures.

Note: R_simple = E/grad_S is algebraically identical to mean(cosine_sims)/std(cosine_sims), i.e., a signal-to-noise ratio. Whether a well-constructed SNR is 'tautological' or 'explanatory' is a philosophical question, not an empirical one.
